name: Wild Mushroom Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 pound mixed fresh wild mushrooms (such as oysters, portobello, cremini)
3 tablespoons chopped fresh herbs (save tips for garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Don't wash mushrooms; brush off dirt or pine needles.
2. Separate the stems from the caps of the wild mushrooms and reserve the tops.
3. Put the stems in a processor with the button mushrooms and process until minced.
4. Add oil, shallots, stock, and processed mushrooms to a frying pan.
5. Bring the liquid to a boil, cover the pan, and reduce heat to low.
6. Simmer for 5-6 minutes or until the mushrooms have shrunk to half their size.
7. Transfer to a strainer over a soup pan and strain out the stock.
8. Press the mushrooms to get all the liquid you can; discard pulp.
9. Slice or tear the reserved tops of remaining uncooked mushrooms into bite-sized pieces and add with cream and tarragon to the liquid.
10. Cook on a low boil until the soup has thickened slightly.
11. Add remaining sherry and cook for 2-3 more minutes.
12. Correct seasoning with salt, if necessary.
